title: The Elder Ground Where Teaching Takes Hold

features.name: the-standing-students
features.desc-on-touch: They are still here, arranged in the earth itself. What they know roots deeper each season. You can feel generations of waiting.

features.name: rings-of-teaching
features.desc-on-touch: Concentric growth-marks: each ring is a year someone learned and then taught. The wood remembers every turn.

features.name: the-survivor-s-shadow
features.desc-on-touch: Longer than it should be, cast by a sun that rose elsewhere. This ground holds what people learned from not-dying.

features.name: manuscript-turned-to-soil
features.desc-on-touch: The pages have returned to the earth. Words dissolve into the teaching that grows here. The students read what the ground writes.

features.name: the-cycle-visible
features.desc-on-touch: A tower of generations: each one stood here, learned, took what they survived, built the next platform. Still building.

features.name: the-ground-as-witness
features.desc-on-touch: This soil has absorbed sweat, determination, the tears of people learning what they didn't think they could bear. The earth holds all of it.

inscription: the students who survived became the ground where the next students learn

reverie.title: REVERIE — the high country teaching ground, late afternoon
narr: You're sitting beside her on the bench. The students are
leaving—the day's class done. She watches them go with
something like recognition. 'They're all teaching something',
she says. 'Even the ones who think they're still learning.'
The ground settles under your feet. It knows what she means.

file.name: Cycle_Of_Becoming_Teacher.txt
file.note: Field notes: what those who survived brought back when they learned to teach. Generations marked in rings. Not healing—hardening, deepening, becoming soil.

exits.label: back downward to where the new students come
higher, where teachers become the taught
toward other grounds marked by survival
into the forest before the cycle began
